name: Mechanical Engineering
description: A comprehensive program covering design, manufacturing, thermal sciences, and industrial engineering. Prepares students for careers in automotive, aerospace, manufacturing, energy, and robotics industries.

subjects.name: Engineering Mechanics
subjects.description: Fundamentals of statics and dynamics including force systems, equilibrium, friction, kinematics, and kinetics of particles and rigid bodies. Covers work-energy and impulse-momentum principles.
learning_outcomes: Analyze forces in static equilibrium for various structural systems
Solve kinematics and kinetics problems for particles and rigid bodies
Apply work-energy and impulse-momentum methods to dynamic systems
Analyze friction forces in mechanical systems
subjects.industry_relevance: Foundation for all mechanical engineering disciplines including structural, automotive, and aerospace design.

subjects.name: Strength of Materials
subjects.description: Stress, strain, and deformation analysis of structural members under axial, torsional, bending, and combined loading. Covers Mohr's circle, beam deflection, column buckling, and strain energy methods.
learning_outcomes: Calculate stresses and deformations in axially loaded members and shafts
Draw shear force and bending moment diagrams for beams
Analyze beam deflections and column buckling loads
Apply Mohr's circle for combined stress transformation
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for structural analysis, machine component design, and failure analysis in all mechanical industries.

subjects.name: Thermodynamics
subjects.description: Laws of thermodynamics, properties of pure substances, ideal and real gas behavior, thermodynamic cycles, and exergy analysis. Covers applications to power cycles, refrigeration, and heat pumps.
learning_outcomes: Apply first and second laws of thermodynamics to closed and open systems
Analyze power cycles including Rankine, Brayton, and Otto cycles
Evaluate thermodynamic properties using tables and equations of state
Perform exergy analysis for energy system optimization
subjects.industry_relevance: Core discipline for power plant design, HVAC, automotive engines, and aerospace propulsion systems.

subjects.name: Fluid Mechanics
subjects.description: Properties of fluids, fluid statics, kinematics, and dynamics including Bernoulli's equation, flow measurement, viscous flow, pipe networks, boundary layers, and turbomachinery fundamentals.
learning_outcomes: Analyze fluid statics and calculate forces on submerged surfaces
Apply Bernoulli's equation and conservation laws to fluid systems
Design pipe networks considering friction and minor losses
Analyze boundary layer behavior and flow separation
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for pump and turbine design, pipeline systems, aerospace aerodynamics, and hydraulic systems.

subjects.name: Manufacturing Technology
subjects.description: Conventional and modern manufacturing processes including casting, forming, welding, machining, powder metallurgy, and additive manufacturing. Covers process selection, tooling, and quality considerations.
learning_outcomes: Select appropriate manufacturing processes for given components
Design casting and welding procedures for defect-free production
Analyze metal forming processes and their limits
Evaluate additive manufacturing technologies for rapid prototyping
subjects.industry_relevance: Backbone of all manufacturing industries including automotive, aerospace, consumer goods, and heavy equipment.

subjects.name: Machine Design
subjects.description: Design of machine elements including shafts, bearings, gears, springs, couplings, clutches, and fasteners. Covers failure theories, fatigue analysis, and design for static and dynamic loading.
learning_outcomes: Design machine components using failure theories and safety factors
Perform fatigue analysis for components under cyclic loading
Design gears, bearings, and shafts for power transmission
Select appropriate materials and manufacturing processes for machine elements
subjects.industry_relevance: Core competency for product design, automotive component design, and heavy machinery manufacturing.

subjects.name: Theory of Machines
subjects.description: Kinematics and dynamics of mechanisms including four-bar linkages, cams, gears, flywheels, governors, and balancing of rotating and reciprocating masses. Covers vibration analysis fundamentals.
learning_outcomes: Perform kinematic analysis of planar mechanisms using graphical and analytical methods
Design cam profiles for specified follower motions
Analyze gear trains for speed and torque ratios
Balance rotating and reciprocating masses in machinery
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for automotive powertrain, robotics, textile machinery, and any mechanism design.

subjects.name: CAD/CAM
subjects.description: Computer-aided design and manufacturing including 3D modeling, assembly, drafting, finite element analysis basics, and CNC toolpath generation. Covers parametric modeling and product lifecycle management.
learning_outcomes: Create parametric 3D solid models and assemblies
Generate engineering drawings with GD&T
Perform basic finite element analysis for design validation
Generate CNC toolpaths for machining operations
subjects.industry_relevance: Industry standard for product design, tooling design, and manufacturing engineering across all sectors.

subjects.name: CNC Technology
subjects.description: Computer Numerical Control technology including CNC machine construction, programming (G-code), tooling, work holding, and advanced machining strategies. Covers multi-axis machining and CNC maintenance.
learning_outcomes: Write and optimize G-code programs for CNC mills and lathes
Select cutting tools and parameters for various materials
Implement multi-axis machining strategies for complex parts
Troubleshoot common CNC programming and machining issues
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for precision manufacturing in aerospace, automotive, mold-making, and medical device industries.

subjects.name: Heat Transfer
subjects.description: Mechanisms of heat transfer: conduction, convection, and radiation. Covers steady and transient conduction, forced and natural convection, heat exchanger design, and thermal radiation analysis.
learning_outcomes: Solve steady and transient conduction problems in various geometries
Analyze forced and natural convection heat transfer
Design shell-and-tube and compact heat exchangers
Calculate radiation heat transfer between surfaces
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for HVAC, automotive thermal management, power plant design, and electronics cooling.

subjects.name: Refrigeration and Air Conditioning
subjects.description: Vapor compression and absorption refrigeration systems, psychrometry, load calculations, air conditioning system design, and refrigerants. Covers components like compressors, condensers, evaporators, and expansion devices.
learning_outcomes: Design vapor compression refrigeration systems for given cooling loads
Perform psychrometric analysis and design air conditioning systems
Calculate heating and cooling loads for buildings
Evaluate refrigerant properties and environmental impact
subjects.industry_relevance: Core discipline for building HVAC, cold chain logistics, food processing, and automotive climate control.

subjects.name: Automobile Engineering
subjects.description: Design and analysis of automotive systems including chassis, suspension, steering, braking, transmission, and engine systems. Covers vehicle dynamics, aerodynamics, and modern automotive electronics.
learning_outcomes: Analyze vehicle dynamics including ride, handling, and stability
Design suspension and steering systems for given performance targets
Evaluate engine performance and emission characteristics
Understand automotive electrical and electronic systems
subjects.industry_relevance: Directly applicable to automotive OEMs, tier-1 suppliers, motorsport, and electric vehicle companies.

subjects.name: Robotics
subjects.description: Robot kinematics, dynamics, and control including forward and inverse kinematics, trajectory planning, manipulator design, sensors, actuators, and robot programming. Covers industrial robot applications.
learning_outcomes: Solve forward and inverse kinematics for serial manipulators
Plan robot trajectories considering joint limits and obstacles
Program industrial robots for manufacturing applications
Design end-effectors and grippers for specific tasks
subjects.industry_relevance: Growing rapidly with industrial automation, warehouse robotics, surgical robots, and collaborative robots.

subjects.name: Mechatronics
subjects.description: Integration of mechanical systems, electronics, control systems, and computing for intelligent product design. Covers sensors, actuators, microcontrollers, and real-time control of mechatronic systems.
learning_outcomes: Design mechatronic systems integrating mechanical and electronic components
Select sensors and actuators for mechatronic applications
Implement real-time control algorithms on microcontrollers
Develop embedded software for intelligent mechanical systems
subjects.industry_relevance: Core to smart products, automotive systems, consumer electronics, and industrial automation.

subjects.name: Industrial Engineering
subjects.description: Work study, method study, work measurement, facility layout, material handling, production planning, inventory management, and operations research. Covers lean manufacturing and Six Sigma principles.
learning_outcomes: Apply work study techniques for productivity improvement
Design efficient facility layouts and material handling systems
Apply operations research techniques for production optimization
Implement lean manufacturing and Six Sigma methodologies
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for manufacturing operations, supply chain, and process improvement across all industries.

subjects.name: Quality Control and Metrology
subjects.description: Statistical quality control, control charts, acceptance sampling, total quality management, and metrology techniques. Covers measurement standards, limits and fits, and dimensional measurement instruments.
learning_outcomes: Apply statistical process control using control charts
Design acceptance sampling plans for quality assurance
Perform precision measurements using mechanical and optical instruments
Implement TQM principles and quality management systems
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for quality assurance in automotive, aerospace, medical device, and precision manufacturing industries.

subjects.name: Production Planning and Control
subjects.description: Production planning, forecasting, aggregate planning, MRP, scheduling, capacity planning, and inventory control. Covers JIT, ERP systems, and supply chain management fundamentals.
learning_outcomes: Develop production plans using forecasting and aggregate planning techniques
Design MRP and ERP systems for manufacturing organizations
Create production schedules considering capacity constraints
Optimize inventory levels using EOQ and safety stock models
subjects.industry_relevance: Core function in all manufacturing companies for efficient resource utilization and on-time delivery.

subjects.name: Metrology and Instrumentation
subjects.description: Advanced metrology techniques including laser interferometry, CMM, surface finish measurement, optical metrology, and in-process gauging. Covers measurement uncertainty and calibration standards.
learning_outcomes: Operate coordinate measuring machines for precision inspection
Apply laser interferometry for linear and angular measurements
Evaluate surface finish parameters using profilometers
Perform uncertainty analysis and calibration of measuring instruments
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for high-precision manufacturing in aerospace, automotive, and semiconductor equipment industries.

subjects.name: Hydraulics and Pneumatics
subjects.description: Design of hydraulic and pneumatic systems including pumps, valves, actuators, circuits, and control systems. Covers fluid power symbols, circuit design, troubleshooting, and electro-hydraulic systems.
learning_outcomes: Design hydraulic circuits for industrial applications
Design pneumatic circuits for automation systems
Select pumps, valves, and actuators for given specifications
Troubleshoot fluid power systems and optimize performance
subjects.industry_relevance: Widely used in heavy machinery, manufacturing automation, construction equipment, and aerospace systems.
